systemOverview.executiveSummary: Build Lite is a Node 24 ESM toolset whose core is a set of local filesystem operations over a project's RCF documents. CRUD, query, view, the build loop and the MCP surface are all thin layers over a shared document store that reads and writes the on-disk rcf tree and validates against the published schemas at every boundary.
systemOverview.systemPurpose: Run the full RCF method over in-repository documents and surface it through deterministic, structured operations for a non-engineering owner and for AI tooling.
systemOverview.architecturalApproach: Layered. A document-store core owns load, validate and persist. A CLI binary, a view renderer, a query engine, a build adapter and an MCP server are all consumers of that core. No layer reaches the filesystem except through the store.
keyCapabilities: Filesystem-backed RCF document store with schema validation at boundaries
Deterministic CRUD verbs over every document type
Diagram and HTML rendering of the whole tree
Traceability and query over the chain
Local stdio MCP surface over the whole feature set

architecturePrinciples.name: Filesystem is the source of truth
architecturePrinciples.description: Every read and write goes to the local rcf tree; no other store mirrors it.
architecturePrinciples.rationale: Guarantees the in-repository differentiator and keeps the tool harness-agnostic.

architecturePrinciples.name: Validate at every boundary
architecturePrinciples.description: Documents are validated against the published schemas on load and before persist.
architecturePrinciples.rationale: A non-coder cannot catch a malformed document by eye; the tool must refuse to write invalid state.

architecturePrinciples.name: Adapters are thin
architecturePrinciples.description: CLI, MCP and the build loop carry no business logic the core does not already own.
architecturePrinciples.rationale: One behaviour, many surfaces; no drift between how a verb behaves on the CLI versus over MCP.

architecturePrinciples.name: Harness-agnostic
architecturePrinciples.description: The tool runs the same against any AI harness because behaviour lives on the local filesystem and not in a hosted surface.
architecturePrinciples.rationale: Survives a change of AI harness without re-implementation or migration.

integrationArchitecture.apiDesign: The store exposes a typed-by-convention internal API of document operations. The CLI maps verbs to those operations and renders results for a terminal. The MCP server maps tools to the same operations and returns structured results. No HTTP API is exposed.
integrationArchitecture.eventModel: None. Operations are synchronous filesystem reads and writes.

operationalConcerns.healthChecks: Not applicable for a local binary; a validate command reports project health by validating every document and listing broken references.
operationalConcerns.logging: Human-readable terminal output by default; a quiet flag for scripting and a verbose flag for diagnostics.
